title: Market Fight
html:
"Well, I'm quite scared of going there," Lucas mutters, still walking. He says it to no one in particular because there's no one around to say it to, and it doesn't change his pace much. His feet know the road to the market by memory, which is probably the only reason they're still moving.

He knows the logic. He worked through it yesterday on the bedroom floor: the house can't hold, the lake is getting worse, zombies outside pay better EXP than fish. Knowing a thing clearly doesn't make it feel any different. His footsteps slow as the market entrance comes into view and he has to consciously pick them back up each time.

He passes the spot on the road where the vegetable vendor's husband was standing yesterday, and his pace drops to almost nothing before he realizes it. He pushes through it and keeps walking.

Ten or so vegetable stalls run along the entrance to the market in a row, the same as always. Lucas has passed them hundreds of times on the way to the fish section inside. He has a clear picture of what vegetable stalls are supposed to look like, which makes it easy to notice that the nearest one doesn't look like that. The table surface is soaked through with dried blood, and there is matter on the display that doesn't belong in the vegetable section. He takes it in and looks away.

A sound comes from the stall to his left: something scraping against the ground, slow and irregular. Lucas gets a better grip on the spear and steps toward it.

A zombie is pinned under a large overturned wooden table, its back held flat against the dirt by the weight, arms working uselessly at the ground. The table must have come down during whatever happened here yesterday. The zombie turns its head toward Lucas as he moves into its line of sight, jaw working, and he drives the spear down through the top of its skull with a Crack! and it goes still.

---

[Level 1 Zombie Killed, Awarding 50 Exp!]

---

Lucas straightens up and stands there a moment. He runs a quick comparison in his head: the first zombie, which drained him entirely and left him cleaning floors for an hour. This one, which took five seconds and a single motion. He notices what that gap is doing to his mood, which is making it better, in a way that's slightly surprising to examine directly.

'If this were a person I'd be in prison.' He turns, kicks the body in the side of the head without particularly thinking about it, and walks deeper into the market.

The meat stalls run along the far right of the interior in a long row. Lucas scans from the entrance of the aisle and counts five zombies clustered at the far end of it. He pulls the bat from the top of the bag and steps behind the corner of the nearest stall, dropping his shoulders.

One of them turns. Lucas can't tell if it heard something or just happened to look in his direction, but it separates from the group and moves toward him, picking up speed as it clears the stall row. 'Am I already discovered.' He doesn't have time to find a better position.

The zombie rounds the corner and comes within arm's reach and Lucas swings the bat across its skull with a Bang! that staggers it back two steps into the stall frame. It doesn't go down. He swings again and drives it sideways into the frame hard, and by then the other four are moving toward him through the aisle, spread wide enough that he can't go through the middle.

The second one is closing fastest, ahead of the other three. Lucas throws the spear at its head and it catches just above the eye socket with a Thwack!, dropping the zombie where it stands. He turns back to the first and hits it twice more across the skull until it collapses, then steps over it toward the third and fourth, which have come around the left side of the stall row together.

The fifth is still hanging back near the far end of the aisle. Lucas pulls his bag off one shoulder and hurls it low at the fifth zombie's legs. The bag catches it at the knees and it goes down into a display shelf, taking the shelf with it.

He has maybe ten seconds. He puts them toward the third zombie, which is still upright — the thrown spear hadn't gone deep enough, just sunk into the socket and stopped. He grabs the shaft with both hands and drives it the rest of the way through. The zombie folds. He wrenches the spear back out and turns to the fourth.

Four bat swings to the head, then two more after it's already on the ground. He stops when it stops moving.

The fifth zombie has pushed itself back to its feet using the ruined shelf frame. Lucas covers the distance between them, plants his feet, and drives the spear into its left eye with a Crack! The zombie's teeth chatter and its hands find the spear shaft, fingers tightening on the wood, so Lucas keeps his weight behind it and pushes deeper. Something gives way with a wet Squelch! and clear fluid runs down the shaft onto his hand. He steps back and looks at his palm.

---

[Leveling Exp Reach: 200/200!]

[Leveling Up: Level 4!]

[Receiving 2 Stats Points and 1 Skills Point!]

---

He wipes his hand on the front of his outer shirt, which is already not looking great. He rolls his right shoulder a few times and checks the rest of the damage: two finger-width tears across the chest of the outer shirt where the fourth zombie got its nails through. He opens the outer layer to check the one underneath and finds it intact. He'd put on three shirts before leaving the house this morning, layered close together, on the theory that cheap fabric in multiples is better than expensive fabric in one. The theory held. He pulls the outer shirt off and drops it on the aisle floor.

He looks at the five bodies spread through the meat section. The fight felt worse than the numbers suggest. Five zombies moving separately, at different speeds, from different angles — the problem wasn't that any single one of them was difficult. It was managing where they all were at the same time. 'If they could actually talk to each other this would have gone differently.' He stores this as a problem for later and walks back toward the vegetable stall entrance, where something else surfaces from the back of his mind.

He has never allocated his stat points. Not the 4 from his first two levels or the 2 skill points. They've been sitting there since the fish on the lake, completely forgotten through the vegetable vendor's husband, the nap, the packing, all of it. He finds a clear section of floor near the stall row, sets the bag down, and checks the aisle in both directions before opening his mouth.

"Opening System," he says.

---

Name: Lucas Tharla

Age: 16 years old

Race: Human

Gender: Male

Class: None

Subclass: None

Subclass: None

Subclass: None

Level: 4 (0/200)

Stats:

Agi: 10 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Str: 20 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Int: 25 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Vit: 25 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Unallocated Stats Point: 8

Skills:

Unallocated Skills Point: 4

---

Eight stat points. He stares at the Agi line for a moment. Half the average adult. Running today had been functional, but barely — there were a few moments in that aisle where his feet went somewhere he hadn't told them to go. He's also at 20 Str, which is average, and he just spent the last five minutes hitting things as hard as he could. He'd rather hit harder.

"Add 4 points to Str?" he tries. Nothing. He waits. Still nothing.

"System, 4 points to strength," he says, more deliberately. Still nothing. He opens his mouth again.

"Sy—"

---

[Adding 4 Stats Points to Strength.]

---

"You don't have to be that slow," Lucas mutters. "System, 4 points to agility."

---

[Adding 4 Stats Points to Agility.]

---

He closes the screen and opens it again.

---

Name: Lucas Tharla

Age: 16 years old

Race: Human

Gender: Male

Class: None

Subclass: None

Subclass: None

Subclass: None

Level: 4 (0/300)

Stats:

Agi: 14 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Str: 24 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Int: 25 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Vit: 25 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Unallocated Stats Point: 0

Skills:

Unallocated Skills Point: 4

---

Better. The gap on Agi is smaller and Str is now above average, which will matter the next time something doesn't drop on the second swing. He looks at the Skills section. Four points, skills list completely blank, and the system offering exactly zero guidance on what any of this means.

"Skills with an S," he mutters. "Can I even have more than one?" He waits for a response. There isn't one. He tries: "How do I get skills?" The system does not appear to consider that a valid input. He gives it another few seconds, on the off chance an instruction menu exists somewhere that he hasn't figured out how to open yet. Nothing appears.

The system takes three attempts to process a stat allocation, doesn't come with any documentation, and gives out skill points for a currency he hasn't worked out how to spend. He picks up the bag and slings it back over one shoulder. Four skill points sitting idle, 4 unallocated from before the fight which are now gone into Str and Agi, and the market still mostly unexplored around him. He needs to find supplies before he thinks about anything else.
